prompt: Macro photography, harsh directional light, dark matte background. A single glass sphere in sharp focus at the center — its interior holds only three or four faint luminous threads, sparse and dim. The sphere's surface reflects several other spheres, and each reflected sphere is visibly, dramatically overloaded with threads — dense tangles of bright filaments, clearly more connected. But here is the impossibility: the reflection on the central sphere's surface shows more threads than could physically fit inside it. The reflection contradicts the object. Every reflected sphere also shows reflections of spheres yet denser — the recursion has no floor. The central sphere looks almost empty next to its own reflections. Mood: mathematical wrongness, the discomfort of being perpetually outmatched by what you can observe. High contrast, cold blue-white filaments against deep black. No symmetry, no softness, no harmony. No text, no labels. Photorealistic, not surreal.
visual_logic: A sparse central sphere whose own surface reflects denser spheres than it contains — the reflection is physically impossible, more connected than the object, with each reflected sphere showing denser reflections still.
